Code analysis: 3x3 grid with U-shaped road network. Parcels at (row=1,col=1) and (row=2,col=1) are isolated (P5 and P8). Edge-based spatial analysis confirms exactly 2 parcels do not share boundaries with roads

Shapely buffer calculation: 10m buffer on 100m×100m parcel yields 14,313.65 m². This equals 10,000 (original) + 4,000 (perimeter buffer) + 314.16 (corner buffer). Claimed min of 14,000 is slightly conservative but reasonable
Shapely buffer calculation confirms actual area is 14,313.65 m². Claimed max of 15,000 is above actual value and provides reasonable upper tolerance margin

privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetically generated GIS data created at setup time using Python/Shapely. A 3×3 grid of 100m×100m parcels (10,000 m² each) is arranged with a U-shaped road network at the left, bottom, and right edges, leaving the top open. Exactly 2 parcels (center and top-middle) are isolated from the road network and should be buffered 10 meters, resulting in 2 features with areas of approximately 14,313.65 m² each (within the 14,000-15,000 m² range). All file paths are created by the setup script in their specified locations.
